title: FTS MATCH query construction does not escape FTS5 operators (syntax-error DoS / query confusion)
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Security and Access (5) / Functional Correctness (1)

Location: src/memory.rs:377 (memory_search)
  let sanitized_query = format!("\"{}\"", query.replace('"', "\"\""));

Violation: The comment implies this is sanitized, but wrapping a string in double-quotes for FTS5 does not actually handle all operator characters. FTS5's MATCH accepts operators (AND, OR, NOT, NEAR, column filters 'colname:token', prefix '*', parenthesized groups). A user memory_search query containing unbalanced quotes or special characters (e.g., the NUL byte, control chars in content) will still produce rusqlite errors propagated to the caller — a trivial DoS where a single bad search breaks the feature. Worse, if we ever expose memory_search through an MCP tool surfaced to untrusted tool output, query confusion could be used to exfiltrate arbitrary rows via crafted boolean expressions.

This is not classical SQLi (rusqlite binds ?1 as a parameter) but it IS an FTS5 expression injection. The right fix is to escape each whitespace-separated token as a phrase literal OR normalize to a safe subset.

Mandated Refactor:
1. Tokenize the user query on whitespace, strip/replace every FTS5 metacharacter ('"', '*', '(', ')', ':', '-'), and rejoin each token wrapped in quotes.
2. On any parse/query error, return Ok(vec![]) rather than propagating — search is best-effort.
3. Add tests for adversarial queries: empty string, only quotes, NEAR/AND/OR/NOT, column filter syntax, 10 KB input.
4. Rename sanitized_query to phrase_query to accurately describe what it does.
